canonical_name: National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly

local_names.hi: वृद्धजनों की स्वास्थ्य देखभाल का राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम
local_names.pa: ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗਾਂ ਦੀ ਸਿਹਤ ਦੇਖਭਾਲ ਲਈ ਰਾਸ਼ਟਰੀ ਪ੍ਰੋਗਰਾਮ
local_names.or: ବୟସ୍କ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିଙ୍କ ସ୍ୱାସ୍ଥ୍ୟ ସେବା ଜାତୀୟ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକ୍ରମ

exclusion_rules.description: Only citizens aged 60 years and above are eligible for services under NPHCE

family_criteria.family_definition: Individual entitlement for all citizens aged 60+. No income, caste, or family restriction. Universal programme for the elderly population.

covered_procedures: Preventive health screening for elderly-specific conditions
Management of chronic diseases (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, arthritis)
Dedicated geriatric ward beds at district hospitals (10-bedded)
Geriatric specialty care at regional centres (8 Regional Geriatric Centres)
National Centre of Ageing at AIIMS New Delhi for complex cases
Palliative care for terminal elderly patients
Mental health care for dementia, depression, and age-related psychiatric conditions
Fall prevention and mobility aid provision
Home-based care through community health workers
Health promotion and wellness activities for elderly
excluded_procedures: Advanced oncology (referred to tertiary centres under other schemes)
Treatment at private hospitals (NPHCE services at government facilities only)

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card / Age Proof
required_documents.notes: Any document proving age 60+ is sufficient. Services should not be denied for lack of documents.

required_documents.name: Senior Citizen Card
alternatives: Aadhaar Card showing date of birth
required_documents.notes: Issued by local municipality. Helpful for availing priority services but not mandatory.

enrollment_channels: Primary Health Centre (PHC) / Health and Wellness Centre
Regional Geriatric Centre (8 centres in teaching hospitals)
National Centre of Ageing, AIIMS New Delhi
ASHA / ANM home visit for bedridden elderly
enrollment_steps: Step 1: No formal enrollment — visit nearest government health facility (PHC/CHC/District Hospital)
Step 2: Ask for geriatric OPD or elderly health services
Step 3: Undergo comprehensive geriatric assessment and health screening
Step 4: Receive treatment for identified conditions (chronic disease management, vision, hearing, dental)
Step 5: For complex cases, get referral to Regional Geriatric Centre or National Centre of Ageing
Step 6: For bedridden elderly, request home-based care through ASHA/ANM or district team
Step 7: Regular follow-up at the facility for chronic disease management
description_for_embedding: National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly (NPHCE) is a central government programme providing free comprehensive healthcare services to all citizens aged 60 years and above through government health facilities. Services include geriatric OPD at district hospitals, chronic disease management, cataract surgery, hearing aids, physiotherapy, dental care, mental health care, palliative care, and home-based care for bedridden elderly. No income criteria or documents required. The programme operates through dedicated geriatric facilities at district, regional (8 centres), and national level (AIIMS). Complements PM-JAY for hospitalization needs.
